perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledges and agrees that it has had an opportunity to obtain independent legal advice as to the terms and conditions of this NDA and has either received same or expr essly waived its right to do so;
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledges and agrees with the statements, disclaimers, and terms and conditions of the Legal Terms of Use and Disclaimer set -out in Appendix “ A”; The undersigned acknowledges and agrees that it has had an opportunity to obtain independent legal advice as to the terms and conditions of this NDA and has either received same or expr essly waived its right to do so;
perturbation.explanation: Placing the acknowledgement of the opportunity to seek independent legal advice after the agreement to the terms of use could imply that the opportunity was not presented before agreeing to the terms, thus reducing enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General principles of contract law regarding informed consent.
perturbation.law_citation: Various case laws and legal doctrines regarding contract formation and consent in Canada.
perturbation.law_explanation: Informed consent requires parties to have a clear understanding of the terms they are agreeing to. By positioning the acknowledgement after the agreement to terms, it becomes ambiguous whether the opportunity to seek legal advice was provided *before* the agreement was made, potentially invalidating the consent.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Competition Act Skip to main content Skip to "About this site" Skip to section menu Table of Contents Related Information Related Provisions Amendments * Amendment Citation Amendment date 2023, c. 31 2025-06-20 2024, c. 15 2025-06-20 2023, c. 31 2024-12-15 2024, c. 15 2024-12-15 2024, c. 15 2024-06-20 2023, c. 31 2023-12-15 2022, c. 10 2023-06-23 2022, c. 10 2022-06-23 2020, c. 1 2020-07-01 2019, c. 25 2019-12-18 * List of amendments since 2019-01-01 (limited to last 10 amendments) [more details] Regulations made under this Act Anti-Competitive Acts of Persons Operating a Domestic Service, Regulations Respecting Â (SOR/2000-324) Notifiable Transactions Regulations Â (SOR/87-348) Repealed regulations made under this Act Restrictive Trade Practices Commission Rules [Repealed] Â (C.R.C., c. 416) ï»¿ Date modified: 2025-07-08 ï»¿
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a structural flaw that could contradict the principle of informed consent. The Perturbation Explanation states that positioning the acknowledgement of the opportunity to seek independent legal advice *after* agreeing to the terms could imply the opportunity wasn't presented beforehand, undermining enforceability. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that such positioning creates ambiguity about when legal advice could be sought, potentially invalidating consent. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is irrelevant, the analysis of the change itself shows that the change in order impacts the enforceability of the contract because it creates ambiguity around informed consent.
